title: Wild Indian Tahini Curry
description: A fiery Andhra-inspired curry that blends creamy tahini, rich chocolate, and fresh spices for a soothing yet spicy dish perfect for sick days.
instructions: Heat a pan over medium heat and add a tablespoon of oil.
Sauté the diced onion until translucent, about three minutes.
Add minced garlic, grated ginger, and cook for one minute.
Stir in chopped tomatoes, cumin, coriander, chili powder, and salt; cook until tomatoes soften.
Pour in coconut milk, bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Add drained chickpeas and simmer for ten minutes to allow flavors to meld.
In a small bowl mix tahini paste with a few tablespoons of hot curry liquid to thin it.
Stir the tahini mixture back into the curry, adding cocoa powder and garam masala.
Continue simmering, stirring occasionally, until the curry thickens to a sauce consistency, about five minutes.
Serve hot over steamed rice or with warm naan, garnished with fresh curry leaves if desired.
